revision.text:
[[ja:童男山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Donanzan Kofun |image = [[File:Donanzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Donanzan No. 1 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|qid=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}} (Donanzan Kofun Subgroup) |location = Yamaichi, Yamauchi, Yame City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = 33 |coordinates = 13 |coordinates = 32.17 |coordinates = 130 |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 44.90 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 48m
Height: 6.7m |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Includes stone enclosure and hollowed-out stone casket base) |burial goods Found = |notes = |built = Late 6th century |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = Fukuoka Prefectural Historic Site |Cultural Asset = |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Round Kofun |map_dot_label = Donanzan Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Donanzan Kofun''' (童男山古墳, Donanzan No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Yamaichi, Yame City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the kofun in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|qid=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}, specifically within the Donanzan Kofun subgroup. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Fukuoka Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in southern Fukuoka Prefecture, Donanzan Kofun is a large round Kofun constructed on a ridge along the northern bank of the Hoshino River, at the easternmost edge of the Yame Hills. The area comprises 27 Kofuns typeing the Donanzan Kofun Subgroup, with this kofun serving as the principal mound. According to local legend, the name "Donanzan" originates from the story of {{ill|Xu Fu|en|Xu Fu|qid=Q700302|lt=Xu Fu|1=Xu Fu}} bringing "boys and girls" (童男草女) to the area before passing away there. Historical documents from the Edo period reference the kofun, which was already open at the time. Excavations were conducted in 1987 (Showa 62). The mound measures approximately 48 meters in diameter and currently stands 6.7 meters high.Historic Site Intypeation Board. The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber consists of an antechamber, main chamber, and entrance passage, with the latter mostly destroyed. Estimated to be about 18 meters in total length, it is considered a large-map_size stone chamber. Inside the main chamber, a {{ill|stone enclosure|ja|石屋形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone enclosure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with hollowed-out stone casket bases types a U-shaped bier configuration, common in Kyushu. Due to historical looting, no grave goods have been identified. The kofun is believed to date to the late 6th century.[https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/soshiki/4/6/31/3/1454651822268.html Donanzan Kofun] (Yame City website). The kofun area was Historic Site1 as a Fukuoka Prefectural Historic Site in 1956. Based on the legend of Xu Fu, a fire ritual called "Donanzan Fusube" is held annually on January 20 in front of the stone chamber to honor Xu Fu. == Historical Record == * 1749 (Kan'en 2): Mentioned in the ''Kan'enki''. * 1853 (Kaei 6): Detailed in Yano Kazusada's ''Chikugo Shoshi Gundan'', including a diagram map_dot_labeled "Donanzan Grotto" and a legend identifying it as the tomb of {{ill|Tsukushi no Iwai|ja|磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Yame City History Vol. 1|1992|qq=}} * 1928 (Showa 3): Recorded in ''Fukuoka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report'' (first mention of the Xu Fu legend).{{Sfn|Yame City History Vol. 1|1992|qq=}} * 1956 (Showa 31): Designated a Fukuoka Prefectural Historic Site on July 28. * 1970 (Showa 45): Measured survey conducted by Yame City Board of Education, reported in 1971.{{Sfn|Kan no Tani Kiln Cluster|1971|qq=}} * 1987 (Showa 62): Excavation survey during environmental improvements, with a report published in 1988.{{Sfn|Yame City History Vol. 1|1992|qq=}} == burial == [[File:Donanzan Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Donanzan Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Diagram of the stone chamber]] [[File:Donanzan Kofun, ishiyakata 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Diagram of the stone enclosure]] The burial facility consists of a multi-chambered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southwest. The structure includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and an entrance passage, though much of the passage is missing. area are as follows:{{Sfn|Kan no Tani Kiln Cluster|1971|qq=}} * Total length: Current 8.6 meters (estimated restored length: approximately 18 meters) * Rear chamber: Length 4.30 meters, width 3.98 meters (rear) and 3.50 meters (front), height 4.10 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.25 meters, width 2.85 meters (rear) and 2.40 meters (front), height 2.85 meters File:Donanzan Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Rear chamber (view toward back wall)
{{small|Stone enclosure at the back wall.|qq=}} File:Donanzan Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) File:Donanzan Kofun, entrance.jpg|Chamber entrance == Historic Site == === Fukuoka Prefectural Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Donanzan Kofun - Designated on July 28, 1956. == Related Facilities == * Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (Iwai no Sato), Yame City == References == {{small|(References used in this article)|qq=}} * Intypeation Board (Installed by Yame City Board of Education, 1998) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1971|chapter=Donanzan Kofun|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/83943 Kan no Tani Kiln Cluster - Report III]|publisher=Yame City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Kan no Tani Kiln Cluster|1971|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Hamada Nobuya|year=1989|chapter=Donanzan Kofun Cluster|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ref={{Harvid|Donanzan Kofun Cluster (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=Donanzan Kofun Cluster|title=Yame City History Vol. 1|publisher=Yame City|ref={{Harvid|Yame City History Vol. 1|1992|qq=}}|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Donanzan Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/soshiki/4/6/31/3/1454651822268.html Donanzan Kofun] - Yame City website ==References== {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|童男山古墳|version=104172219|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:童男山古墳]] {{Commons category|Donanzan Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q125960589}} [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Xu Fu]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]]
